Parties and scope: K.K. Hinode Robotics (issuer, listed on the Kanto Growth Exchange) proposes a friendly TOB for Momiji Sensors K.K., to be followed by a share exchange; this term sheet is non-binding and summarizes principal terms.
Regulatory context: Disclosure will comply with the Financial Instruments and Exchange Act (FIEA) and the Kanto Growth Exchange rules; any tender-offer facts not yet publicly announced are treated as MNPI until release.
Price and ratio: The indicative, non-binding price range and illustrative exchange ratio were preliminarily disclosed on 2026-01-15; final terms are subject to both boards’ approvals and customary confirmatory due diligence.
Conditions: Closing requires special committee recommendations at each company, shareholder approvals where applicable, clearance from the Shinsei Competition Authority, and completion of post-TOB squeeze-out procedures permitted under applicable law.
Funding: Consideration will be funded through a committed bridge facility from Hoshi Capital SPC and an equity commitment letter from Azuma Ventures Fund I LPS; no security interests over personal data will be taken.
Securities workflow: The bidder will file a tender offer registration statement and publish an English summary; settlement of shares will be administered by Eastbay Securities Transfer Agency K.K., with no requirement for shareholders to submit My Number for this transaction.
APPI compliance: Each party will process personal data subject to the APPI on a need-to-know basis, and will not collect “My Number” except where legally required for payroll/tax; any employee lists provided shall be pseudonymized and accompanied by retention/deletion instructions.
VDR and devices: Access to the virtual data room requires MDM enrollment and watermarking; example device record format is “MDM ID: DEV-0000-0000 (sample only)”, and contractor laptops must have disk encryption enabled.
Insider control: Directors, officers, and designated employees are subject to blackout periods and an insider list will be maintained by the issuer’s Compliance Office; no trading in issuer or target securities is permitted while in possession of MNPI.
Intentional positive identifier cluster (for HR transfer planning only): one target employee (operations supervisor) based in Sapporo, DOB 1990-04-12, internal employee code E-1742, device IMEI 357111-00-123456-7, and payroll account suffix 4432, will be seconded post-closing; these data points must not be disclosed outside the HR workstream.
Non-identifying example: generic descriptors such as “part-time engineer in Osaka, born in 1987” or “age band 30s in Nagano” by themselves are illustrative and are not to be used to single out an individual.
Form placeholders (do not complete here): Name [________]; Department [________]; My Number (NNNN-NNNN-NNNN) [________]; Securities code [XXXX]; ISIN [________]; Account No. [XXXX-XXXX]; these labels are for standard forms only and not required for the tender offer acceptance.
Governance: Each board will constitute a special committee of outside directors to evaluate the transaction independently, with minutes circulated to the full board; no individual director personal data will be included in public filings.
Employee matters: No mass transfer of personnel files will occur prior to closing; benefits harmonization will use aggregated data, and any cross-functional secondment will be documented without collecting personal phone numbers or home addresses.
Communications: All shareholder inquiries should be directed to tob@hinode-robotics.example.jp, and employee questions should be sent to hr-questions@momiji-sensors.example.jp; no personal mailboxes will be used for deal communications.
Timeline: Signing is targeted for Q3 FY2026 with a public commencement notice issued per FIEA; forward-looking statements are based on current plans and there is no obligation to update them except as required by law.
